Milestone 4 — Auth & Profile Management

Red highlights in screenshots are editorial annotations showing the area of interest for each step. They are not part of the application's interface.

  • US-5.1: User Login
  • US-5.2: Profile Submission with Admin Approval

US-5.1

User Login

**As a** user, **I can** log in to access personal features (profile editing for researchers, admin panel for admins).

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN the login screen is displayed, WHEN the user enters valid email/password and clicks "Se connecter," THEN they are authenticated and redirected to the dashboard.
  • GIVEN the user is logged in, WHEN the navbar renders, THEN the "Connexion" button is replaced by their avatar (initials) and name, with a dropdown menu.
  • GIVEN the user is logged in as admin, WHEN the navbar renders, THEN an "Admin" tab appears and an "Admin" badge is shown next to the name.
  • GIVEN the user dropdown is visible, WHEN they click "Mon profil," THEN they navigate to their own profile.
  • GIVEN the user dropdown is visible, WHEN they click "Deconnexion," THEN they are logged out and the navbar reverts to showing the "Connexion" button.

Happy Path

1
Action: The user views the application before logging in
Expected: A 'Connexion' button is visible at the right of the navbar
2
Action: The user clicks the 'Connexion' button in the navbar
Expected: A login card appears with email, password fields and demo buttons
3
Action: The user views the login card on the login page
Expected: The login card shows email input, password input, 'Se connecter' button, and two demo buttons
4
Action: The user enters valid credentials and clicks 'Se connecter'
Expected: Login succeeds and the user is redirected to the dashboard, showing 'Marie Dupont' in the navbar
5
Action: The user observes the navbar after a successful login
Expected: The 'Connexion' button is replaced by the user's avatar initials and name 'Marie Dupont'
6
Action: The user clicks on their name in the navbar
Expected: A dropdown opens with 'Mon profil' and 'Deconnexion' options
7
Action: The user clicks 'Mon profil' in the dropdown
Expected: The user's own profile page loads showing their name, bio, keywords, and publications
8
Action: The user returns to the dashboard and clicks the user area again
Expected: The dropdown reopens with 'Mon profil' and 'Deconnexion' options
9
Action: The user clicks 'Deconnexion' in the dropdown
Expected: The user is logged out and the navbar reverts to showing the 'Connexion' button
1
Action: The user navigates to the login page
Expected: The login card is displayed with email, password fields, and demo login buttons
2
Action: The user clicks the 'Demo Admin' button on the login page
Expected: The admin is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard with an 'Admin' tab and badge visible
3
Action: The user observes the navbar after admin login
Expected: An 'Admin' tab is visible in the navbar and an 'ADMIN' badge appears next to the user name
4
Action: The user clicks the 'Admin' tab in the navbar
Expected: The administration page loads with a placeholder note that the full panel is available in M5

Edge Case E1: Invalid Credentials

GIVEN invalid credentials are entered, WHEN the user clicks "Se connecter," THEN an error "Email ou mot de passe incorrect" appears below the form.

1
Action: The user navigates to the login page to test invalid credentials
Expected: The empty login form is displayed
2
Action: The user enters 'bad@email.com' and a wrong password, then clicks 'Se connecter'
Expected: The login fails and an error message 'Email ou mot de passe incorrect' appears in red below the fields
3
Action: The user observes the form after a failed login attempt
Expected: The error message 'Email ou mot de passe incorrect' remains visible below the input fields

Edge Case E2: Auth API Unreachable

GIVEN the auth API is unreachable, WHEN the user submits login, THEN an error "Service indisponible, reessayez plus tard" is shown.

1
Action: The user views the login form while the authentication service is unavailable
Expected: The login form is displayed with credentials filled in
2
Action: The user clicks 'Se connecter' while the authentication service is blocked
Expected: The request fails and the error 'Service indisponible, reessayez plus tard' appears in red
3
Action: The user observes the form after a service failure
Expected: The error 'Service indisponible, reessayez plus tard' is shown below the form fields

Edge Case E3: Session Expired

GIVEN the user's session token expires, WHEN they perform an action, THEN they are redirected to the login screen with a message "Session expiree, veuillez vous reconnecter."

1
Action: The user logs in successfully before a session expiry is simulated
Expected: The dashboard loads with the user 'Marie Dupont' shown in the navbar
2
Action: The session expiry interception is set up while the user is on the dashboard
Expected: The dashboard appears normally before any further action triggers the expired session
3
Action: The user navigates to another page after the session has expired
Expected: The researchers page shows 'Une erreur est survenue' with a 'Reessayer' button, indicating a failed API call
4
Action: The system detects the expired session and redirects the user
Expected: The login page appears with the message 'Session expiree, veuillez vous reconnecter' in red
US-5.2

Profile Submission with Admin Approval

**As a** logged-in researcher, **I can** submit my profile which will be validated by an administrator before publication.

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN a logged-in researcher views their own profile, WHEN the "Modifier" button is displayed, THEN it is enabled and clickable.
  • GIVEN a logged-in researcher views another's profile, WHEN the "Modifier" button is displayed, THEN it is disabled with a lock icon and a note "Vous ne pouvez modifier que votre propre profil."
  • GIVEN a user is not logged in, WHEN they view a profile, THEN the "Modifier" button is hidden entirely.
  • GIVEN a researcher submits their profile, WHEN the submission succeeds, THEN it appears in the admin "Profils en attente" list.

Happy Path

1
Action: The user logs in as Marie Dupont (researcher)
Expected: The dashboard loads with 'Marie Dupont' visible in the navbar
2
Action: The user navigates to their own profile page
Expected: Marie Dupont's profile page loads with bio, keywords, publications, and a 'Modifier' button
3
Action: The user observes the 'Modifier' button on their own profile
Expected: The 'Modifier' button is enabled and clickable with no lock icon
4
Action: The user clicks the 'Modifier' button on their profile
Expected: The profile edit form loads with pre-filled name, lab, keywords, bio, and publications
5
Action: The user views the edit form for their profile
Expected: A yellow banner appears at the top stating the profile will require admin approval before being published
6
Action: The user modifies their bio and clicks 'Enregistrer'
Expected: A green success toast 'Profil enregistre avec succes.' appears at the bottom right
1
Action: The user logs in as Marie Dupont to test viewing another researcher's profile
Expected: The profile edit form is displayed with the bottom of the form and save/cancel buttons visible
2
Action: The user navigates to Ahmed Benali's profile page
Expected: Ahmed Benali's profile loads with a locked 'Modifier' button and note that only your own profile can be edited
3
Action: The user observes the 'Modifier' button on another researcher's profile
Expected: The 'Modifier' button is shown with a lock icon and the note 'Vous ne pouvez modifier que votre propre profil'
1
Action: A non-logged-in user navigates to a researcher's profile
Expected: The profile page loads without any 'Modifier' button visible
2
Action: The anonymous user observes the profile page edit area
Expected: No 'Modifier' button is present on the profile page for unauthenticated visitors

Edge Case E1: Already Pending Submission

GIVEN a researcher submits while their previous submission is still pending, WHEN they try to submit again, THEN a warning "Vous avez deja une soumission en attente" is shown.

1
Action: The user opens the profile edit form while an 'already pending' response is configured
Expected: The profile edit form is displayed with the approval banner and pre-filled fields
2
Action: The user submits the profile form when a pending submission already exists
Expected: The form is submitted but the API returns a pending error
3
Action: The user sees the result after submitting a profile with a pending submission
Expected: A pink warning banner 'Vous avez deja une soumission en attente' appears above the form fields

Edge Case E2: Save API Failure

GIVEN the submission API fails, WHEN the researcher clicks save, THEN an error appears and the form data is not lost.

1
Action: The user views the profile edit form while the save API is configured to fail
Expected: The profile edit form loads normally with all fields pre-filled
2
Action: The user fills the form and clicks 'Enregistrer' while the server is unavailable
Expected: The save fails and a red error toast 'Erreur de sauvegarde, veuillez reessayer.' appears
3
Action: The user observes the form after a save failure
Expected: The red error toast 'Erreur de sauvegarde, veuillez reessayer.' is visible and all form data is preserved

Edge Case E3: Admin Rejection Notification

GIVEN an admin rejects a profile, WHEN the researcher logs in, THEN they see a notification "Votre profil a ete rejete" with the admin's reason.

1
Action: The user's profile is loaded with a rejection status injected by the test
Expected: The profile page shows a red banner 'Votre profil a ete rejete' with the admin's rejection reason
2
Action: The user logs in and navigates to their own profile after an admin rejection
Expected: The profile page displays with a rejection notice banner at the top indicating the reason given by the admin
3
Action: The user observes the rejection notification on their profile
Expected: The red banner reads 'Votre profil a ete rejete. Raison : Informations insuffisantes, veuillez completer votre biographie.'
US-2.3

Add/Edit Profile Form

**As a** logged-in researcher, **I can** add or edit my own profile via a structured form, pending admin approval.

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN a logged-in researcher navigates to the form, WHEN it loads, THEN fields are pre-populated with existing data (for edit) or empty (for add).
  • GIVEN the form is displayed, WHEN the user types a keyword and presses Enter, THEN a new tag is added to the keyword input.
  • GIVEN the form is displayed, WHEN the user clicks "+ Ajouter une publication," THEN a new blank publication block (title, co-authors, venue) appears.
  • GIVEN the user fills the form, WHEN they click "Enregistrer," THEN the profile is submitted for admin approval and a success toast appears.
  • GIVEN the form is displayed, WHEN the user clicks "Annuler," THEN they return to the researcher list without saving.
  • GIVEN the form loads, WHEN displayed, THEN a yellow approval banner reads "Votre profil sera soumis a validation par un administrateur avant d'etre publie."

Happy Path

1
Action: The user logs in and navigates to the profile edit form
Expected: The edit form loads with name, lab, keywords, bio, and publications pre-filled, with an approval banner
2
Action: The user views the full edit form including all its fields
Expected: The form displays all sections: name, lab dropdown, keywords tags, biography textarea, and publication blocks
3
Action: The user types 'NLP' in the keywords input and presses Enter
Expected: A new 'NLP' tag is added alongside the existing keywords in the tag area
4
Action: The user clicks the 'x' on the 'alignement' keyword tag to remove it
Expected: The 'alignement' tag is removed and only 'conformance', 'process mining', and 'NLP' remain
5
Action: The user clicks '+ Ajouter une publication'
Expected: A new blank 'Publication 3' block appears with empty title, co-authors, venue, and year fields
6
Action: The user fills in the new publication fields with title, co-authors, venue, and year
Expected: The publication block shows 'Process Mining with NLP', Sophie Leclerc, CAiSE 2024, 2024
7
Action: The user clicks 'Enregistrer' to save the updated profile
Expected: A green success toast 'Profil enregistre avec succes.' appears at the bottom right
8
Action: The user returns to the form and clicks 'Annuler'
Expected: The user is navigated back to their profile page without saving any changes

Edge Case E1: Required Fields Empty

GIVEN required fields are empty, WHEN the user clicks "Enregistrer," THEN validation errors highlight the missing fields and submission is blocked.

1
Action: The user opens the profile edit form to test required field validation
Expected: The edit form loads with all fields filled and the approval banner at the top
2
Action: The user clears the 'Nom complet' field and clicks 'Enregistrer'
Expected: The name field is empty and focused, triggering validation
3
Action: The user observes the form after submitting with the name field empty
Expected: The name field is highlighted in red with the message 'Ce champ est requis.' below it

Edge Case E2: Save API Failure

GIVEN the save API fails (500), WHEN the user submits, THEN an error toast "Erreur de sauvegarde, veuillez reessayer" appears and the form data is preserved.

1
Action: The user views the edit form while the save endpoint is configured to return a server error
Expected: The profile edit form is displayed normally with all fields pre-filled
2
Action: The user fills in the form and clicks 'Enregistrer' while the server is failing
Expected: The save fails and a red error toast 'Erreur de sauvegarde, veuillez reessayer.' appears
3
Action: The user observes the form after a save API failure
Expected: The red error toast is shown and all form data remains intact, including the name, keywords, bio, and publications

Edge Case E3: Duplicate Keyword

GIVEN the user adds a duplicate keyword, WHEN they press Enter, THEN the duplicate is rejected with a brief "Mot-cle deja present" note.

1
Action: The user opens the profile form that already has the tag 'conformance' present
Expected: The edit form loads with existing keywords 'conformance', 'alignement', and 'process mining' visible
2
Action: The user types 'conformance' in the keywords input, which already exists as a tag
Expected: The text 'conformance' is visible in the keyword input field alongside the existing tags
3
Action: The user presses Enter to add the duplicate keyword 'conformance'
Expected: The tag is not added and a red message 'Mot-cle deja present' appears below the keywords field

Known Limitations

  • The `already_pending` warning relies on frontend mock interception — no backend DB constraint enforces single-pending submissions yet (planned for M5 admin approval workflow)
  • US-5.2 E3 rejection banner test assertions are stubbed (conditional on DB state) — feature works visually but lacks automated regression protection
  • Lab dropdown in edit form queries the researchers table (not a dedicated labs table) — works but new labs require at least one existing researcher entry
  • Cancel button navigates to the researcher's profile page instead of the researcher list (minor UX deviation from spec)

What's Next

  • M5 — Administration: User management (roles, invitations, approve/reject pending profiles), bulk import (CSV/Excel + Google Scholar), app settings (language, similarity threshold, NLP algorithm), audit logs with date filters and color-coded action tags
